metric_definitions.research_accuracy: Token overlap between predicted and ground-truth industry labels.
metric_definitions.pain_point_accuracy: Per GT pain point, max of token-overlap match and embedding-cosine paraphrase match (text-embedding-3-small; full credit >= 0.60 cosine, zero <= 0.35, linear between). Mean over GT pain points.
metric_definitions.persona_accuracy: Role-family overlap between predicted buyer enum and GT persona text.
metric_definitions.email_quality: Weighted composite: company name (20%), pain coverage (40%, token or embedding-cosine vs email sentences), reference facts (25%, token or whole-email cosine), CTA (15%).
metric_definitions.reviewer_agreement: Agreement with expected reviewer decision derived from research + email quality.

notes: Official n=100 OutboundBench baseline (2026-07-18). Ground truth is human-authored from real web evidence (Firecrawl/Tavily scrapes). Pain-point and email scoring combine exact token overlap with embedding-cosine paraphrase matching (text-embedding-3-small; full credit at >=0.60 cosine, zero at <=0.35, linear between), so semantically-equivalent phrasing earns credit instead of scoring zero on vocabulary differences. ICP accuracy reflects the ICP agent scoring against an explicit SellerProfile (config/seller_profile.json); the benchmark's icp_target is a generic seller-agnostic heuristic, so the two answer subtly different questions. Persona (49%) remains the primary product gap: coarse 8-value buyer enum vs rich natural-language ground-truth personas.
